titel: Revocation of permanent residence permits and adaptation of Swedish law to the EU's migration and asylum pact.
html:
The government is implementing a paradigm shift in Swedish migration policy. The goal is to reduce asylum-related immigration to sustainable levels to create better conditions for integration and to reduce social exclusion. An important part of this work is to align Swedish legislation on the granting of international protection and asylum procedures with the EU's minimum legal standards. In the proposal, the government therefore suggests that the possibility for certain foreigners to be granted permanent residence permits should be removed from the Aliens Act (2005:716).

The proposal also includes suggestions for adapting Swedish law to the EU's migration and asylum pact. These proposals include the following:

  • The terminology and structure of the Swedish asylum procedure will be changed.
  • The so-called border procedure for asylum will be applied in more situations than those that are mandatory according to EU law.
  • The right to publicly funded legal assistance in the handling of cases concerning international protection at the Swedish Migration Agency will be limited.
  • The police authority, the Swedish Migration Agency, and the regions are responsible for carrying out the new screening procedure.
  • The age limit for taking fingerprints and photographs in certain cases during the migration process will be lowered to six years.

The legislative changes are proposed to come into force on July 12, 2026, with the exception of the changes in the new reception law, which is proposed to come into force on October 2, 2026.

Proposal for a Parliamentary Decision

Government's Proposals:

  1. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposal for an amendment to the Aliens Act (2005:716).
  2. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposal for an amendment to the Parental Code.
  3. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposal for an amendment to the Social Security Code.
  4. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposal for an amendment to the Act (1990:52) on special provisions regarding the care of young people.
  5. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposal for an amendment to the Population Registration Act (1991:481).
  6. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposal for an amendment to the Act (1991:1128) on compulsory psychiatric care.
  7. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposal for an amendment to the Act (1991:1129) on forensic psychiatric care.
  8. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposal for an amendment to the Act (1994:137) on the reception of asylum seekers, etc.
  9. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposal for an amendment to the Student Support Act (1999:1395).
  10. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposal for an amendment to the Act (2001:82) on Swedish citizenship.
  11. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposal for an amendment to the Act (2001:454) on the processing of personal data within social services.
  12. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposal for an amendment to the Act (2005:429) on a legal guardian for unaccompanied minors.
  13. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposal for an amendment to the Act (2008:290) on approval for research institutions to receive visiting researchers.
  14. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposal for an amendment to the Act (2008:344) on healthcare for asylum seekers, etc.
  15. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposal for an amendment to the Public Access and Secrecy Act (2009:400).
  16. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposal for an amendment to the Planning and Building Act (2010:900).
  17. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposal for an amendment to the Act (2013:156) on orientation programs for certain newly arrived immigrants.
  18. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposal for an amendment to the Act (2013:407) on healthcare for certain foreign nationals residing in Sweden without the necessary permits.
  19. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposal for an amendment to the Aliens Data Act (2016:27).
  20. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposal for an amendment to the Act (2016:38) on the reception of certain newly arrived immigrants for settlement.
  21. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposal for an amendment to the Act (2017:584) on responsibility for integration measures for certain newly arrived immigrants.
  1. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposal for an amendment to the Act, Prop. 2025/26:262 (2018:1693), on the police's processing of personal data within the area of the criminal data act.
  2. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposal for an amendment to the Act (2022:700) on special control of certain foreign nationals.
  3. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposal for an amendment to the Act (2024:79) on the placement of children in sheltered housing.
  4. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposal for an amendment to the Act (2024:691) on the registration of foreign nationals with temporary protection in certain cases.
  5. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposal for an amendment to the Act (2025:945) amending the Aliens Data Act (2016:27).
  6. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposal for an amendment to the Reception Act (2026:000).
  7. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposal for an amendment to the Act (2026:000) amending the Act (2023:339) amending the Act (2018:1693) on the police's processing of personal data within the area of the criminal data act.
  8. The Parliament shall approve the government's proposal for an amendment to the Act (2026:000) amending the Act (2025:947) amending the Act (2018:1693) on the police's processing of personal data within the area of the criminal data act.
tldr: The government calls it a paradigm shift in migration policy. The possibility of obtaining permanent residency is being removed from the immigration law, and Swedish regulations are being aligned with the EU's migration and asylum pact, as well as the EU's legal minimum standards for protection. The goal is to reduce immigration related to asylum.
lattlast:
The government describes the proposals as a paradigm shift in migration policy. The goal is to reduce the number of asylum seekers to what the government calls sustainable levels, in order to improve integration and reduce marginalization.

A key proposal is to remove the possibility for certain foreign nationals to obtain permanent residency from the Aliens Act.

In addition, Swedish law will be adapted to the EU's migration and asylum agreement. This includes changes to the terminology and the system used in the Swedish asylum procedure to align with the EU's common minimum standards.
